subject: Geography
title: Who Owns the Poles?
description: A short lesson to help you apply Who Owns the Poles?.

hook.text: Nobody owns Antarctica. The Antarctic Treaty of 1959 reserves it for peace and science.

buildup.text: Twelve nations signed the treaty. Today, 54 countries are party to it.

discovery.text: Military activity, mining, and nuclear testing are all banned on the continent.

twist.text: Seven countries still maintain territorial claims—but the treaty freezes them indefinitely.

climax.text: Antarctica is the only continent governed by international cooperation, not national sovereignty.

punchline.text: One continent belongs to everyone—and therefore no one.

quiz.question: What does the Antarctic Treaty reserve the continent for?
